definition:
ἀντί-τυπος, -ον
 (see: τύπος), [in LXX: Est.3:13 A * ;] 
  1. active striking back; metaphorically, resisting, adverse. 
   2. Pass. struck back; metaphorically, corres­ponding to (MM, VGT, see word); 
   (a) as impression of a seal or copy of an archetype (τύπος) (RV, Like in pattern), Heb.9:24; 
   (b) as the reality (of which τύπος is the copy or adumbration) (RV, after a true likeness), 1Pe.3:21 (Cramer, 357).†
 (AS)
